name: H-IIA
first: Japan's longest-serving operational launcher — flew Hayabusa, Akatsuki, and Hope Probe
description: Mitsubishi Heavy Industries' two-stage workhorse, in service von 2001 through retirement in 2024. Cryogenic core stage with 2-4 solid boosters. Notable interplanetary Missionen: Hayabusa (2003) and Hayabusa2 (2014) nach asteroids; Akatsuki (2010) nach Venus; the UAE's Hope Probe (2020) nach Mars; SLIM (2023) nach the Mond. Replaced by the H3.
